title: The Coastal Convergence Point

features.name: the tide reader's column
features.desc-on-touch: A standing stone marked with coordinates that shift as you watch. The tide rises and falls, and with it, the numbers rearrange themselves as if the ocean were learning mathematics.

features.name: the convergence marker
features.desc-on-touch: Here the grid lines and the water lines learned to occupy the same space. Neither map nor tide wins; they compromise on the sand.

features.name: the saltwater archive
features.desc-on-touch: A series of stone troughs where the tide pools each number from each grid, measuring them with brine and time.

features.name: the measuring ground
features.desc-on-touch: Sand marked with angles and arcs. The tide erases them; the archive returns them each dawn. Neither wins—they have learned to repeat each other.

features.name: the horizon memory
features.desc-on-touch: Where water meets the recorded edge of every map ever made. The horizon is not a line; it is a list of coordinates reaching back to where archives began.

features.name: the tidal calculation pool
features.desc-on-touch: Water so shallow you can see the numbers engraved on the bedrock beneath. Each tide adds new figures; subtraction happens when the moon forgets to pull.

inscription: coordinates and tides learned to read each other in the space where difference becomes a form of agreement

file.name: Tidal_Survey_1984_Waterlogged.txt
file.note: Maritime archive's tide tables — corrupted: each entry now contains the time when the coordinate was made, smeared like salt stain

exits.label: back north toward the grid where numbers still believe they are separate from water
further into the tidal reach where coordinates dissolve into pure current
eastward along the coast where every point looks back at every other point
inland where coordinates remember they were paddocks first
